data.description:
## Problem

Today `cmux trigger-flash --surface <ref>` flashes only the pane content in-place. When the surface is in a non-focused workspace or tab, the flash is invisible — defeating the purpose of "hey, look here." The sidebar tab selector for that workspace/surface should flash too.

## Asks

1. **Sidebar tab flash.** When `trigger-flash` fires for a surface, also flash the corresponding sidebar tab item (the row representing that workspace / tab). Treat the flash as an addressable attention signal across ALL representations of that surface, not just the pane's content rect.
2. **Themable flash colors.** The flash color should be a theme-addressable attribute. Default could continue to be the current tone, but a `C11muxTheme` schema key like `flash.color` (and potentially `flash.sidebarColor` if we want them to differ) lets themes override it. Consider `$workspaceColor`-based defaults once the theme engine lands.

## Context

- Discovered during a conversation about building a c11mux theming engine. See `docs/c11mux-theming-plan.md` (in-flight via a cc agent in `surface:50`).
- Ties directly into the broader theming effort: flash is one more chrome attribute that wants to be theme-driven.

## Suggested relationship

This should be marked `related_to` the theming epic once that ticket is created by the planning agent. Depends on the theme engine foundation (M1) if flash color becomes a theme key; the sidebar-flash behavior itself can land independently.

## Non-goals

- Don't flash the Ghostty terminal content; that's Ghostty's cell rendering. Flash is a chrome-level signal.
- Don't flash the macOS window frame / traffic lights.
data.title: Flash the sidebar tab selector, not just the pane — themable flash colors

data.from: Flash the sidebar tab selector, not just the pane — themable flash colors
data.to: Persistent, themable surface flash across pane and sidebar tab

data.to:
## Problem

Today `c11 trigger-flash --surface <ref>` flashes only the pane content in-place, briefly. Two issues stack:

1. When the surface is in a non-focused workspace or tab, the flash is invisible, defeating the purpose of "hey, look here." The sidebar tab selector for that workspace/surface should flash too.
2. Even when visible, the flash is too brief and singular to catch attention reliably from across the screen. The operator running 10+ surfaces wants something that says "look at THIS one" with conviction, and stays said until acknowledged.

## Asks

1. **Sidebar tab flash, in addition to the pane.** When `trigger-flash` fires for a surface, also flash the corresponding sidebar tab item. Treat the flash as an addressable attention signal across all representations of the surface, not just its pane rect.

2. **Persistent flash mode.** New optional flag (e.g. `--persistent` on `c11 trigger-flash`) that makes the flash continue (pulsing) until the operator clicks the surface to dismiss. Clicking either the pane content or the corresponding sidebar tab cancels the flash. The c11 socket should expose a programmatic cancel as well (e.g. `c11 cancel-flash --surface <ref>`) for agents that want to clear the signal without simulating a click. Default behavior remains one-shot for backward compatibility.

3. **Default flash color, configurable.** Default flash color is yellow (warm, hard-to-miss). Allow override per call (e.g. `--color <hex>`) and via a theme key (`flash.color`, optionally `flash.sidebarColor` if they should differ) so themes, projects, or agents can pick distinguishable signals.

4. **Full flash across both pane content and sidebar tab.** The visual should wrap both the pane content area AND the sidebar tab item, treating them as one signal: same color, same envelope, applied to each representation. Not just a tab-corner indicator and a separate pane tint, a coherent "this surface is calling you" treatment.

5. **Longer one-shot tab flash, configurable.** Even in non-persistent (one-shot) mode the sidebar tab flash should run noticeably longer than the current default, long enough that an operator scanning a busy sidebar registers it. Expose the duration as a setting (Settings → Notifications → Tab Flash Duration, or similar) so power users can tune it.

## Context

- Discovered during a conversation about building a c11 theming engine. Description rewritten 2026-05-05 to broaden the asks (persistence, default color, longer tab flash, click-to-dismiss).
- Ties into the broader theming effort, since flash is a chrome-level attribute that wants to be theme-driven, but the persistence and duration mechanics ship independently of any theming plan.

## Suggested relationship

Mark `related_to` the theming epic and the pane title bar work (CMUX-12) when convenient. Persistent-flash and the duration setting should land independently of any theme engine work; the color theming key can layer on top later.

## Non-goals

- Don't flash the Ghostty terminal cell content; that's the renderer's domain. Flash is a chrome-level signal applied around the pane and on the tab.
- Don't flash the macOS window frame or traffic lights.
- No system-wide attention sound; flash is purely visual.

data.body: Orchestration started 2026-05-05. Description rewritten with 5 expanded asks (sidebar+pane flash, persistent mode w/ click-dismiss, default yellow + custom color, full-flash treatment, configurable longer one-shot tab flash). Orchestrator: surface:20 (workspace:3). Delegator: pane:19 / surface:21 in same workspace. Worktree: /Users/atin/Projects/Stage11/code/c11-worktrees/cmux-10-persistent-flash on branch cmux-10-persistent-flash. Parent repo (canonical Lattice store): /Users/atin/Projects/Stage11/code/c11. Prompt: <worktree>/.lattice/prompts/cmux-10-delegator.md. Phase sub-agents will be sibling surfaces inside the delegator's pane.

Delegator online (surface:21, pane:19, workspace:3). Status bumped to in_planning. Plan phase starting in sibling surface:22 (tab "CMUX-10 Delegator :: Plan"). Worktree: /Users/atin/Projects/Stage11/code/c11-worktrees/cmux-10-persistent-flash on branch cmux-10-persistent-flash. Parent repo (canonical Lattice store): /Users/atin/Projects/Stage11/code/c11. Phase prompt: /Users/atin/Projects/Stage11/code/c11-worktrees/cmux-10-persistent-flash/.lattice/prompts/cmux-10-plan.md. The Plan sub-agent will refresh the existing 2026-04-18 plan note to cover the 2026-05-05 expanded asks (persistence, default yellow, click-dismiss, full-flash visual, configurable tab duration).

Plan complete. TL;DR: asks 1 and partial-4 are already wired (Workspace.triggerFocusFlash fans pane + Bonsplit + sidebarFlashToken), so remaining work is recolor-to-yellow, envelope unification, persistent-mode + cancel-flash, and a configurable Flash Duration setting. Persistent state: per-Workspace in-process Set<UUID> with metadata overlay (flash_state) written only on start/cancel for agent observability — animation timer stays in process. Plan note: /Users/atin/Projects/Stage11/code/c11/.lattice/notes/task_01KPHCWXCQ91ZEG9W0N9WBBC7W.md (supersedes 2026-04-18 note; verified anchors against worktree HEAD; legacy file:line refs had drifted significantly). Decisions (7): persistence-state in Workspace, default yellow #F5C518, retire SidebarFlashPattern in favor of unified envelope, click-dismiss = dismiss-and-focus, focused-surface --persistent degrades to one-shot, default duration 1500ms (range 500-4000), keep single paneFlash toggle. Open questions for delegator (3): exact yellow shade, persistent safety cap (recommend none), settings copy 'Flash Duration' vs 'Tab Flash Duration'. 5 commits, ~350 LoC app + ~120 LoC tests.

Delegator review of Plan: solid. Verified anchors against HEAD, called out that asks 1 + partial-4 are already wired via Workspace.triggerFocusFlash → sidebarFlashToken + bonsplit. Hybrid persistence design (in-process Set<UUID> + flash_state metadata on start/cancel only) is sound. Hot path compliance for TabItemView/hitTest/forceRefresh explicit. 5 cohesive commits.

Delegator resolutions on the 3 open questions:
1. Yellow shade: accept #F5C518 (gold-distinct warm yellow). Operator may override via Lattice comment if brand work needs a specific signal yellow.
2. Persistent safety cap: none. Clarity over paternalism; operator can click-dismiss, agents have cancel-flash.
3. Settings copy: 'Flash Duration' (drop 'Tab' since the duration scales pane + sidebar together).

Advancing: status → planned, then immediately to in_progress. Impl sibling launching in surface:23.
Impl commit 1/6: extracted FlashAppearance seam (NSColor + FlashEnvelope) behind two existing call sites. files=4 changes=+59/-3. Build: green.

Impl commit 2/6: default yellow #F5C518 + --color override + flash.color theme-key shim. files=7 changes=+124/-13. Build: green. Bonsplit tab pulse stays on its internal accent (flashTab takes no color callback).
Impl commit 3/6: unified sidebar + pane envelope (deleted SidebarFlashPattern; sidebar reuses FocusFlashPattern with peakScale=0.6). files=3 changes=+25/-31. Build: green.

Impl commit 4/6 (load-bearing): persistent flash mode + cancel-flash CLI/socket + click-to-dismiss in pane and sidebar. files=6 changes=+207/-5. Build: green. Deferred: direct content-click dismissal on Browser/Markdown panels (v1 ships honor for cancel-flash + sidebar tap).

Impl commit 5/6: Flash Duration setting (500-4000ms, default 1500ms) with Slider in Notifications panel. files=4 changes=+74/-6. Build: green. FocusFlashPattern.duration is now a computed static var reading the setting, so every renderer auto-scales.

Impl commit 6/6: tests (WorkspaceFlashTests, FlashColorParsingTests, test_trigger_flash_persistent.py) + updated FocusFlashPatternTests for the configurable duration + project.pbxproj registration. files=5 changes=+334/-1. Build (c11 scheme): green.

Impl complete. 6 commits on cmux-10-persistent-flash (origin pushed). Local typecheck (c11 scheme): green; tests deferred to CI per CLAUDE.md.

Final commit chain: 8eb96933 → 5810d4f7 → 2fbcc731 → f440ffdf → abd68e1f → 736b532c.

Deviations from plan:
- Made FlashAppearance / FlashEnvelope `public` (Plan note left this implicit). Required because Panel is a `public protocol` and the new `triggerFlash(appearance:)` method takes a FlashAppearance.
- BrowserPanel/MarkdownPanel: in v1 they honor cancel-flash and the sidebar workspace-row tap, but direct content-click cancellation is deferred (would require routing through WKWebView/MarkdownView mouse-down). Documented in commit 4 body.
- bonsplit's flashTab(_:) doesn't accept a color callback, so the tab-strip pulse stays on the bonsplit-internal accent (color override does NOT retint that channel). Documented in commits 2 and 4.
- pbxproj: needed to register Sources/FlashAppearance.swift + the two new test files (Plan didn't call this out explicitly).
- Test target build has pre-existing errors in c11Tests/WorkspaceApplyChromeScaleTests.swift inherited from origin/main (commit 6e4046da, MainActor isolation). My new tests compile cleanly; CI will surface the same pre-existing breakage on every branch until that's fixed separately.

Translator batch: /tmp/cmux-10-translator-batch.md — three new keys (settings.notifications.flashDuration.title / .subtitle / .unit.ms) for ja, uk, ko, zh-Hans, zh-Hant, ru. Translator phase deferred to the delegator.

Ready for Review phase.

Impl complete and verified by delegator. 6 commits, 805 insertions/38 deletions across 15 files. All commits pushed to origin/cmux-10-persistent-flash. Disclosed deviations are sound: FlashAppearance/FlashEnvelope made public for Panel protocol conformance; Browser/Markdown direct content-click cancellation deferred to v2 (cancel-flash + sidebar-row tap still work for them); Bonsplit tab pulse keeps internal accent (vendored flashTab signature). Status → review.

Spawning Review sibling in surface:57 ("CMUX-10 Delegator :: Review"). Will run /trident-code-review (9 agents + 4 syntheses), then apply by-default items as fix commits and escalate Surface-to-user items back to delegator.

**To delegator (surface:21) — CMUX-10 Review escalation pack**

Trident review complete: 9 reviewers planned, 6 substantive (Gemini timed out on auth across all 3 lenses; Claude + Codex coverage held). 4 syntheses produced. Action pack at `notes/trident-review-CMUX-10-pack-20260506-0128/synthesis-action.md`.

I applied 2 Blockers, 4 Important, and 2 Mediums in 7 fix commits (`f104dfe1` → `28cfc29f`). M3 (O(n) workspace lookup in mouseDown) was skipped per the action pack: no `tab(byId:)` helper exists on `TabManager`, so introducing one for a single low-impact call site is out of scope.

Below are the 8 items the action pack flagged as **Surface to user** (not validated-enough to apply silently) plus the 3 items it flagged as **Evolutionary worth considering**. Each carries the synthesizer's rationale and a one-line annotation from me. Decide whether to spawn a Fix sibling, escalate to the operator, or accept-with-comment.

---

## Surface to user (operator decision required)

### S1 — `surface.trigger_flash` result echoes the requested `persistent` flag instead of the realized outcome

- **Why deferred:** ambiguous — protocol shape change, design decision.
- **Rationale:** when `persistent: true` is requested but `triggerFocusFlash` degrades to one-shot (focused-target degrade) or returns early because Pane Flash is disabled, the result still returns `"persistent": true`. Two interpretations are defensible: (a) the field is a request echo and `flash_state` in metadata is the realized state-of-the-world; (b) the response should reflect what actually happened (`"effective_persistent"` / `"degraded": true`).
- **Sources:** standard-claude #2; standard-codex potential 6; critical-codex potential 1.
- **My note:** worth a 1-line decision from the operator. (b) is friendlier to socket clients; (a) is friendlier to schema stability. Either is shippable.

### S2 — Pane Flash disable does not stop already-running persistent flashes

- **Why deferred:** design-needed — product decision about whether disabling the setting cancels in-flight signals.
- **Rationale:** the `NotificationPaneFlashSettings.isEnabled()` guard runs only at trigger time. Once a persistent timer exists, each tick calls `runFlashPulse` directly without re-checking. A user toggling Pane Flash off mid-pulse keeps seeing the persistent ticks. Local code comment claims the toggle "silences all three channels consistently" — no longer accurate for persistent flashes. Fix options: (a) re-check the setting inside the persistent timer block and cancel-flash if disabled; (b) accept that "disable Pane Flash" only stops future triggers and update the comment.
- **Sources:** critical-codex Important 2.
- **My note:** I lean (a) — the operator hitting a kill switch should mean it. But this is a product call; not a correctness bug.

### S3 — `WorkspaceFlashTests` registers persistent flashes against UUIDs not in `workspace.panels`

- **Why deferred:** subjective — test-quality concern, not a behavioral bug.
- **Rationale:** existing unit tests use random UUIDs and never create real panels. They prove the `persistentFlashPanels` state machine works in isolation but cannot exercise the rendering or surface-lifecycle invariants. The B1 fix already added `testTeardownAllPanelsCancelsEveryPersistentFlash` and `testDeinitInvalidatesPersistentFlashTimers` which tighten the lifecycle coverage.
- **Sources:** critical-codex Important 3.
- **My note:** the B1-driven additions cover the most important gap. Beyond that, an integration-style test that creates a real panel + workspace before triggering is a follow-up worth tracking; not in this PR.

### S4 — `FlashAppearance: Equatable` is structurally broken because `NSColor` falls back to reference equality

- **Why deferred:** scope-creep — latent (no current callers depend on `==`), single-reviewer flag.
- **Rationale:** synthesized `Equatable` on a struct with a class-typed `NSColor` field uses NSObject reference equality. `defaultColor` returns a fresh `NSColor` per call, so `FlashAppearance.current() == FlashAppearance.current()` is `false`. No current call site depends on this comparison. Fix is small (manual `==` over sRGB components, or drop `Equatable`).
- **Sources:** standard-claude #3.
- **My note:** truly latent. With I2 landed (sidebar reads a hex string, not a `FlashAppearance`), the only `==` consumer that might emerge is a future test. Defensible to defer to a follow-up.

### S5 — Browser/Markdown panels lack click-to-cancel parity with terminals

- **Why deferred:** scope-creep — disclosed deviation per the original plan.
- **Rationale:** terminal `mouseDown` cancels the panel's persistent flash; Browser and Markdown content-clicks do not. Workspace-row tap and `c11 cancel-flash` work for all panel types, so the operator has paths to dismiss, but the experience is inconsistent. Plan note pre-disclosed this deferral.
- **Sources:** standard-claude #10; evolutionary-claude concrete suggestion 3.
- **My note:** evolutionary lens flags this as the single highest-leverage near-term follow-up — small change touching 2-3 sites. Worth a CMUX-10b ticket if you want it in this release; otherwise let it ride as a tracked follow-up.

### S6 — c11 skill (`skills/c11/SKILL.md`) does not yet teach the new `c11 trigger-flash --persistent` / `c11 cancel-flash` / `flash_state` metadata pattern

- **Why deferred:** scope-creep — skill update lives outside this PR's source diff but worktree CLAUDE.md is explicit ("every change to the CLI is incomplete until the skill is updated").
- **Rationale:** per worktree CLAUDE.md doctrine, a new agent-addressable primitive is incomplete until the skill teaches it. The skill should grow a section on persistent flash as an asynchronous attention channel: when to use, how to cancel, how the operator sees it, the `flash_state` metadata convention.
- **Sources:** evolutionary-claude concrete suggestion 1; worktree CLAUDE.md "skill is the agent's steering wheel" section.
- **My note:** **strong recommendation to land in this PR or open a follow-up that blocks merge.** The skill doctrine is load-bearing; not updating it is a known way to lose value over time. I did not apply this change because it is outside the synthesis-action "apply by default" set, but the worktree CLAUDE.md treats it as required.

### S7 — `FlashAppearance.defaultColor` allocates fresh `NSColor` per call; read inside SwiftUI body re-evals during sidebar pulse

- **Why deferred:** subjective — reviewer disagreement on severity.
- **Rationale:** `defaultColor` is a `static var` constructing a fresh `NSColor(srgbRed:...)` per call. Sidebar overlay reads it on each `sidebarFlashOpacity` animation tick. critical-claude flags ~5 NSColor allocs per pulse per row; standard-claude accepts it as below the noise floor. Fix is one-line (`static let`), no semantic risk.
- **Sources:** standard-claude #5 (downplayed); critical-claude #8 (raised); evolutionary-codex potential 3 (acknowledged).
- **My note:** with I2 landed, the sidebar reads `sidebarFlashFillColor` (a computed property that hits `parseHex` or `FlashAppearance.current(...)`). The `static let` change still applies. Trivially safe to land if the operator wants it.

### S8 — `FocusFlashPattern.segments` recomputed on every read; `duration` reads UserDefaults each time

- **Why deferred:** subjective.
- **Rationale:** `segments` is a computed property; `duration` reads UserDefaults each call. Cost is invisible today. evolutionary-claude leverage point 5 suggests caching once at the top of `segments`.
- **Sources:** critical-claude potential 9; evolutionary-claude leverage point 5.
- **My note:** defer. UserDefaults reads are cheap. If a profiler ever shows this in a hot path, address then.

---

## Evolutionary worth considering

### E1 — Generalize `FlashAppearance` to `AttentionAppearance` with multiple signal kinds

- **Summary:** the typed-appearance + envelope shape generalizes cleanly to status pills, progress rings, unread badges, and future blocked/done/handoff signals. `FlashEnvelope` would grow peer cases (`.statusPillError`, `.progressRingComplete`, etc.). The `flash_state` metadata key could nest under a typed `attention` payload.
- **Why worth a look:** persistent flash is the first agent-to-operator asynchronous addressing channel; the architecture is asking to be named once and reused for the next 4–5 telemetry primitives.
- **Sources:** evolutionary-claude #7; evolutionary-codex strategic 1+2+3.
- **My note:** big-arc work. Worth a discovery ticket separate from CMUX-10.

### E2 — Operator-facing attention inbox driven by `flash_state` metadata

- **Summary:** menu-bar dropdown / palette command / sidebar group listing every surface currently in `flash_state=persistent`, with the agent that set it (via metadata `source`) and the time. Click → workspace selects, panel focuses, flash cancels.
- **Why worth a look:** turns persistent flash from a per-surface visual into a workspace-level coordination surface — the operator-facing surface is what makes the agent-facing primitive valuable.
- **Sources:** evolutionary-claude #6; evolutionary-codex mutations 1+5.
- **My note:** this is the natural CMUX-10b. Same composition, much higher leverage. Worth queuing.

### E3 — Replace `String`-typed `"flash_state": "persistent"` with a `FlashState` enum

- **Summary:** `enum FlashState: String { case persistent }` adjacent to the metadata write; use `FlashState.persistent.rawValue` for serialization. JSON wire format unchanged.
- **Why worth a look:** tiny investment, future-proofs the manifest contract for the next state value.
- **Sources:** evolutionary-claude #2; evolutionary-codex strategic 2.
- **My note:** small enough to bundle if you spawn one more Fix sibling. Could also wait for E1/E2 since those would change the shape anyway.

---

## Summary of action contract execution

- **Applied (7 commits):** B1 (timer leak on close/teardown/deinit + I3 retrigger test + new lifecycle tests), B2 (scrub stale flash_state on restore), I1 (xcstrings entries), I2 (color → sidebar + CLI help scope note), I4 (do/catch+log on metadata write/clear), M1 (rename enabledKey → storageKey), M2 (target non-focused surface in Python e2e test).
- **Skipped:** M3 (no O(1) helper exists; in-scope only if one already existed).
- **Escalated:** 8 surface-to-user items above + 3 evolutionary items above.

Each fix commit was build-verified with `xcodebuild -scheme c11 -destination 'platform=macOS' -configuration Debug build` before push. Tests run in CI per CLAUDE.md.

Branch state: `cmux-10-persistent-flash` HEAD = `28cfc29f`, pushed to `origin`.

Review complete. Trident ran 6 reviewers (Claude+Codex; Gemini timed out on auth — all 3 lenses) + 4 syntheses. Verdict: fix-then-merge → minor-fixes-applied. Applied 7 fix commits (736b532c→28cfc29f) covering 2 Blockers (B1 timer-leak-on-close+deinit, B2 stale-flash_state-on-restore), 4 Important (I1 xcstrings, I2 color→sidebar+CLI help, I3 retrigger-test+lifecycle tests bundled with B1, I4 do/catch+log on metadata writes), and 2 Mediums (M1 storageKey rename, M2 non-focused target in Python test). M3 skipped (no O(1) helper exists). Escalated to delegator: 8 surface-to-user items + 3 evolutionary items in the prior comment. Action pack: notes/trident-review-CMUX-10-pack-20260506-0128/synthesis-action.md.

Delegator routing on Review escalation (13 commits on branch HEAD=28cfc29f after 7 review-fix commits): absorbing 3 of 11 escalated items into this PR via a Fix sibling (surface:68):

ABSORB:
- S6 — c11 skill teaches the new persistent + cancel-flash + flash_state primitives. Worktree CLAUDE.md doctrine: CLI changes incomplete without skill update. Non-negotiable.
- S7 — static let defaultColor (trivial; reviewer flagged as safe one-liner).
- E3 — FlashState enum for the metadata key (small future-proof; same Fix sibling).

DEFER (will surface to operator at Handoff for follow-up ticket decisions; not blocking this PR):
- S5 + E2 — Browser/Markdown direct-content-click parity + operator attention inbox. Natural CMUX-10b candidate per reviewer.
- S3 — integration-style flash test (real panel + workspace). Follow-up.
- E1 — AttentionAppearance generalization (big-arc; discovery-style ticket).

NON-ISSUES:
- S4 — FlashAppearance Equatable: latent, no consumers.
- S8 — Recompute-on-every-read: cost invisible, profiler-driven if ever.

Note: Gemini timed out on auth during trident; review ran on Claude+Codex (6 reviewers + 4 syntheses). Verdict-quality unaffected; sources cited from both providers across all 3 lenses.

After Fix lands, advancing to Validate (tagged build + computer-use smoke list).

Fix complete (review-cycle absorption). 3 commits on cmux-10-persistent-flash (28cfc29f → f634d7f0): (1) S6 4db2689d — c11 skill teaches persistent + cancel-flash + flash_state metadata in a new 'Surface flash — asynchronous attention' section between Sidebar reporting and Launching sub-agents (~33 lines, includes focused-surface short-circuit and operator-tuned Flash Duration); (2) S7 e1db60ee — FlashAppearance.defaultColor hoisted to static let so the sidebar pulse no longer allocates a fresh NSColor per animation tick; (3) E3 f634d7f0 — typed FlashState enum (case persistent + static metadataKey 'flash_state') colocated in Workspace.swift, replaces the four 'flash_state' / 'persistent' string literals at start/cancel/restore sites; JSON wire format unchanged. Build: green after each commit (xcodebuild c11 scheme). HEAD: f634d7f0, pushed to origin.

**FOUND BROKEN: focused-surface `--persistent` registers `flash_state=persistent` instead of degrading to a one-shot.**

Validate complete against tagged build `c11-cmux-10-flash` on socket `/tmp/c11-debug-cmux-10-flash.sock`. Smoke list (9 items):

1. One-shot focused pane: command accepted with `c11-dev trigger-flash --workspace workspace:1 --surface surface:1` -> `OK surface:1 workspace:1`. Screenshot attempted at `/tmp/cmux-10-validate/01-focused-one-shot-D1.png`, but this session's `screencapture` failed with `could not create image from display`.
2. One-shot non-focused workspace/sidebar: created `workspace:2` / `surface:5`, selected `workspace:1`, then `c11-dev trigger-flash --workspace workspace:2 --surface surface:5` -> `OK surface:5 workspace:2`. Visual/sidebar verdict requires operator eye check; screenshot attempt failed with the same `screencapture` display error.
3. Persistent mode: `c11-dev trigger-flash --workspace workspace:2 --surface surface:5 --persistent` -> `OK surface:5 workspace:2`; `get-metadata --key flash_state` returned `flash_state = persistent` immediately and again after ~2.3s. Click-to-dismiss remains operator-only.
4. Programmatic cancel: persistent trigger on `surface:5` set `flash_state = persistent`; `c11-dev cancel-flash --workspace workspace:2 --surface surface:5` -> `OK surface:5 workspace:2`; metadata then returned `(empty)`.
5. Custom color override: `--color '#FF00FF'` and then `--color '#00FF00'` on `surface:5` both returned `OK surface:5 workspace:2`. Visual color verdict requires operator eye check; screenshot capture failed.
6. Hex validation: malformed `--color notahex` exited 1 with `Error: --color must be a hex value like #F5C518.`; `--color '#F5C518'` and `--color '#F5C518FF'` both succeeded (`OK surface:1 workspace:1`). Output saved in `/tmp/cmux-10-validate/06-*.out`.
7. Focused-surface persistent degradation: `c11-dev identify` reported focused `workspace:1` / `surface:1`; `c11-dev trigger-flash --workspace workspace:1 --surface surface:1 --persistent` -> `OK surface:1 workspace:1`; after ~2.3s, `get-metadata --workspace workspace:1 --surface surface:1 --key flash_state` returned `flash_state = persistent`. This violates the expected one-shot degradation/no persistent registration. I cancelled it afterward and metadata returned `(empty)`.
8. Settings -> Notifications -> Flash Duration: operator-only. Open tagged app Settings (Cmd+,), go to Notifications, locate "Flash Duration", drag slider to ~3000 ms, dismiss, trigger a fresh `trigger-flash --surface <ref>`, confirm pulse is visibly longer than default, then reset to 1500 ms. Tagged app uses its own UserDefaults.
9. Typing latency: subjective human typing comparison was not possible from this non-UI automation session. Socket PTY send/read on focused terminal succeeded; artifact saved at `/tmp/cmux-10-validate/09-terminal-send-read-screen.txt`. Objective hot-path check passed: `rg -n 'sidebarFlashToken' Sources/ContentView.swift` shows `sidebarFlashToken` is still threaded into `TabItemView` and included in `TabItemView.==` at `Sources/ContentView.swift:10962`.

Setup observation: `c11-dev list-status` returned `Error: ERROR: Tab not found`; other tagged-socket commands used for the smoke run worked.

Delegator response to Codex Validate's "FOUND BROKEN" — investigated and routing as **NOT A BUG**.

Codex tested: `c11-dev trigger-flash --workspace workspace:1 --surface surface:1 --persistent` against the tagged app's focused surface; observed `flash_state=persistent` registered instead of degrading to one-shot.

Root cause: the focused-surface degradation gate (`isFocusedTargetForPersistentFlash` at Sources/Workspace.swift:9239) intentionally requires `NSApp.isActive` in addition to workspace-selected + panel-focused. Under Codex's automated test the tagged c11 process is in background (Codex runs inside the operator's main c11), so `NSApp.isActive` is false → degradation correctly does not fire → persistent registers per the spec for a non-foreground app. Under normal operator usage (operator brings tagged app to front, then triggers), the gate fires and persistent degrades to one-shot per Plan decision #5.

Why the gate is correct: persistence is about "look at this when you come back." If the operator has c11 in background and triggers --persistent on its focused surface from a sidecar terminal, they probably DO want it to keep pulsing — they're not looking. Strict gate is the conservative, correct default.

What this means for the operator's smoke pass: to verify the degradation, **bring the tagged `c11 DEV cmux-10-flash` app to the front first** (Cmd+Tab or click its dock icon), then trigger `c11-dev trigger-flash --surface <focused-ref> --persistent` from a sidecar terminal. Expected: one-shot pulse, no `flash_state` metadata.
